reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — Node inference across the STS rubric range, headless first-visit + warm-cache browser runs, desktop + mobile screenshots, responsive matrix

rubric.evidence: Real cross-encoder/stsb-TinyBERT-L4 text-classification (num_labels=1) runs in models/sts-similarity/worker.js. Headless first-visit browser run (WASM, no GPU): clicked Download (~58 MB) → state 'ready' → real score 3.14 for the default pair 'A man is playing a guitar.' ↔ 'A person is playing a musical instrument.' Standalone Node run over the full STS range: identical 4.86, doctor/physician paraphrase 4.55, guitar/violin 1.68, eating/riding-horse 0.62, love/hate negation 1.05, unrelated 0.12 — a correctly graded 0–5 regressor, not a classifier. 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Genuine graded similarity: the negation pair (love vs hate) scores low despite ~85% shared words — the cross-encoder is reading meaning, the demo's central claim.

rubric.evidence: Output shape matches the regression contract: the model emits a single raw logit per pair; worker.js reads it straight off pipe.model (the pipeline would relabel it), then sigmoid → 0–1 → ×5 → 0–5. The 'See inside' panel shows that exact chain (logit → sigmoid → ×5) so the single output → gauge mapping is legible. Batched scoring returns one row per pair for the dedup/multi-model views.
rubric.notes: No thresholding into a yes/no; the honest raw logit is always shown alongside the derived score.

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot confirms the at-a-glance: model id cross-encoder/stsb-TinyBERT-L4 linked to card, task text-classification (num_labels=1, regression), scale explained (sigmoid(logit)×5), architecture 4-layer TinyBERT cross-encoder (STSb ≈ 85.5), quantization fp32 ONNX ~58 MB, backend WebAssembly, license Apache-2.0. Inference off the main thread via worker.js.
rubric.notes: fp32 chosen honestly: this repo ships a standard model.onnx (~58 MB) but its int8 builds use non-standard avx/arm filenames transformers.js dtype:'q8' can't resolve — at 58 MB fp32 is browser+mobile-feasible, so no quality loss is taken to hit a smaller number.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile: state 'download-required', single 'Download model (~58 MB)' action — no silent auto-download. Warm reload in the same profile: state reached 'ready' automatically with the score re-rendered and ZERO Download buttons present (verified programmatically: downloadBtnPresent=false). Uses the shared createModelLoader.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract exactly: absent → labelled Download; current cached → auto-init, no button.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live status region on every page; the shared loader announces checking→downloading→ready with a progress bar during the ~58 MB download; a .status.err surfaces scoring failures on the page; the loader exposes a Retry path on failed init.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and partial-eviction paths are handled by the shared loader but were not separately driven here.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled native controls across the ladder: two sentence textareas, sample chips (min-block-size 2.2rem), a Score button; practical adds a 0–5 threshold range slider (with live value); multi-model adds a shortlist-size range slider. All keyboard-operable native elements; the slider re-runs on input.
rubric.notes: A full driven keyboard-traversal pass (focus order + visible focus on every control at both viewports) is the remaining agent check; layout + operability verified.

rubric.evidence: On a fresh profile the gauge/result area stays hidden and the loader shows the honest 'This model isn't on your device yet.' Download state — nothing canned shown as live output. Every gauge value comes from the on-device run (verified: 3.14 in-browser matched the Node sigmoid×5).

conformance script assertions pass by construction: every button has a text name; no without alt (SVG data-URI favicon only). The 0–5 gauge is a semantic role=meter with aria-valuemin/max/now and an aria-valuetext ('3.14 of 5 — Related, some shared details'), so screen readers get the value AND the band, not just a coloured bar. Colour is paired with a text band label (not colour-only). dir=auto on inputs/pair text.
rubric.notes: Band colour (good/accent/warn) is reinforced by the text label per the highlight/colour-contrast guidance.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth ≤ innerWidth, no clipped controls, console + network clean. Screenshots read: reports/responsive/screens/sts-similarity/{desktop,mobile}.png — the two-input grid (auto-fit minmax) stacks to one column on mobile, gauge ticks/readout stay legible, chips wrap. support flipped to ok on both classes.
rubric.notes: The .sts-inputs and .inside-chain grids use repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)) + min-inline-size:0 to avoid overflow on narrow viewports.

rubric.evidence: All scoring runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) off the main thread, batched in one forward pass for the dedup/multi-model views; the gauge/table render is over a handful of DOM nodes. The slider re-run is debounced by only firing when a result already exists.
rubric.notes: Architecture is INP-friendly; an explicit INP measurement during a large-batch dedup would confirm.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy synchronous work off the main thread (workers) so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all cross-encoder inference runs in models/sts-similarity/worker.js; pairs are batched into a single forward pass; the main thread only renders the gauge/table.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; first-visit run stayed responsive during the ~58 MB download

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + logical properties + minmax auto-fit grids and min-inline-size:0 to avoid horizontal overflow on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .sts-inputs and .inside-chain use grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px/150px, 1fr)); textareas box-sizing:border-box inline-size:100%; chip rows flex-wrap; pair text uses min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Expose meaningful values through semantics (roles/ARIA), pair colour with text, keep names on controls.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the similarity gauge is role=meter with aria-valuemin/max/now + aria-valuetext (value + band); the band colour is always paired with a text label; every button has an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: renderGauge() in sts.js emits role=meter + aria-valuetext; conformance buttons-have-names + images-have-alt pass

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/inference failures in a live region the user can read, not only the console.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — scoring failures set a .status.err in a role=status aria-live region; the shared loader exposes a Retry path.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live in every page; loader error state

openQuestions: Drive a full keyboard traversal at mobile + desktop: are both textareas, every chip, the threshold/shortlist sliders and the Score button reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring?
The 0–5 mapping assumes sigmoid(logit)×5; is that the exact activation the STS-B training used for TinyBERT-L4, or should the see-inside note a small calibration caveat at the extremes?
For the practical dedup view over a long list (n² pairs), at what n does the batched forward pass start to affect INP, and should it be chunked with scheduler.yield()?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a 'similarity matrix' wild demo: score every pair in a small set and render an n×n heatmap of graded 0–5 similarity, contrasting the cross-encoder grid with the bi-encoder cosine grid.
Add an explicit INP measurement to the practical dedup rung for large lists and chunk the batch with scheduler.yield() if it regresses.

summary: sts-similarity is a strong, honest cross-encoder STS demo, distinct from the bi-encoder embedders and the relevance rerankers: it emits a single graded 0–5 similarity by joint-encoding a sentence pair, verified across the full range (identical 4.86 → negation 1.05 → unrelated 0.12) in Node and end-to-end in the browser (Download ~58 MB → ready → 3.14; warm-cache auto-init with no button). Accurate at-a-glance, honest fp32 rationale, a semantic role=meter gauge with aria-valuetext, a legible logit→sigmoid→×5 'see inside', clean mobile+desktop layout (support ok), and a four-rung ladder (basics → practical dedup-with-a-threshold → wild negation/near-paraphrase traps → multi-model bi-encoder-shortlist-then-cross-encoder-rescore). Revision 1.
